segments.s006-g1: A compact, somewhat inverted nominal clause: چراغ است = 'it is a lamp'; مر is the dative/clitic 'for me' placed before the rest; تیره شب را is the definite object/topic 'the dark night' (را marks it); بسیچ is an emphatic, archaic intensifier meaning 'very/greatly' modifying the idea. So literally: 'It is a lamp for me, greatly, the dark night.'
segments.s006-g2: به بد = 'toward bad/evil' (به as 'towards/into'); تا توانی = 'as far as you are able' (تا + توانى here = limit/extent: 'to the extent you can'); تو هرگز مپیچ = negative present-jussive/imperative with prohibitive prefix م‑: مپیچ = 'do not turn/turn away' directed at 'you' (تو) and qualified by هرگز ('never'). The clause as a whole gives an admonition: 'as much as you can, never turn toward evil.'
segments.s006-g3: چو = temporal 'when'; سی روز = 'thirty days' (duration); گردش بپیمایدا is an older/poetic verbal phrase meaning 'has completed/traversed a circuit' (گردش = revolution/circuit, بپیمایدا = archaic perfect/optative sense 'to complete/cover'). Together: 'when (it) has completed a thirty-day circuit.'
segments.s006-g4: شود is the present/future auxiliary 'becomes/happens'; the predicate that follows is a small clause: تیره گیتی = 'the dark world' (subject), بدو = 'to it' (dative pronoun, here referring back to the thing that completed the circuit), روشنا = adverb/adjective 'bright/brightly'. Word order places the auxiliary before the clause: 'the dark world becomes bright to it.'
segments.s006-g5: پدید آید = a two-part verbal expression 'appears/comes into view' (پدید = 'manifest/appear', آید = 'comes'); آنگاه = 'then/thereupon'; باریک و زرد = two coordinated adjectives 'thin/slender and yellow/pale' describing the appearance. The verbs precede the adjectives, a normal poetic order for 'then appears thin and pale.'
segments.s006-g6: چو = 'as/like' introducing a simile; پشت کسی = 'the back of someone' (پشت = back); کو here is an archaic/colloquial relative or particle meaning roughly 'who' (it is a non-standard classical form, not the interrogative 'where'), followed by غم عشق خورد where خورد is used idiomatically to mean 'befell/was consumed by' rather than literal 'eat'. So the whole simile: 'like the back of someone to whom the sorrow of love has befallen.'
segments.s006-g7: چو = 'when' (temporal); بیننده = 'the beholder/viewer' (subject); دیدارش = 'his meeting/meeting with him' (object, with possessive sense); از دور = 'from afar'; دید = past tense 'saw'. The clause is a simple temporal past: 'when the viewer saw the meeting from afar.'
segments.s006-g8: هم = 'also/even' adding emphasis; اندر زمان = 'at the time/moment' (اندر = in/at); او = 'he' (subject); شود = 'becomes' (here rendering a past/resulting sense in the narrative: 'became'); ناپدید = 'invisible/vanished' (predicate adjective). Together: 'at that moment he too became invisible.'
segments.s006-g9: دگر (ḏegar) here means 'again' or 'another' placed before شب 'night' to mark repetition; نمایش کند is a verb phrase with کند (3rd sg. present/habitual) after the noun, a common Classical Persian order where the noun+infinitive/masdar 'نمایش' ('a showing, display') is followed by the light verb کند 'does/makes' to mean 'will show' or 'shows'. بیش‌تر is an adverb 'more' modifying the whole clause and appears after the verb phrase for emphasis.
segments.s006-g10: تو را روشنایی دهد is a simple transitive clause: تو 'you' marked by the object marker را, روشنایی 'brightness/light' is the direct object, and دهد is 3rd sing. present ('gives' / classical present used for future/result). بیش‌تر follows the verb as an adverb 'more, to a greater degree.'
segments.s006-g11: به دو هفته is a temporal phrase 'in/within two weeks' (به here functioning to mark a time span); گردد is the verb (3rd sg. classical present used to express future/result), and تمام و درست are predicative adjectives 'complete and proper' placed after the verb to describe the resulting state.
segments.s006-g12: بدان is here a fused/formal expression meaning 'to that' or 'to that state' (etymologically به + آن); باز گردد is 'will return again' (باز 'again' + گردد 'turn/become/go back'); the relative clause که بود از نخست 'which it was from the first' uses که to introduce the clause, بود as the past copula 'was', and از نخست 'from the beginning.' Note: 'دان' in the word list is not functioning as the lexical verb 'to know' here but part of the contraction (hence no direct sense from that list applies).
segments.s006-g13: بود 'was' is the past copula introducing the state; هر شبانگاه is an adverbial time phrase 'each night / every night,' and باریک‌تر is the comparative adjective 'thinner / narrower' describing how it was each night. The comparative -تر indicates increasing degree over time.
segments.s006-g14: به خورشید تابنده نزدیک‌تر is a prepositional phrase modifying the previous clause: به 'toward/to' + خورشید 'the sun' + تابنده a present-participle adjective 'shining', and نزدیک‌تر the comparative 'nearer/closer' — together 'closer to the shining sun.'
segments.s006-g15: بدینسان 'thus' opens the clause; نهادش is the noun نهاد 'innate nature / disposition' with the third-person possessive suffix -ش 'its'; خداوند is the subject 'God / the Lord' and داد is the past verb 'gave.' Word order puts the object (نهادش) before the subject and verb, a common poetic inversion.
segments.s006-g16: بود until تا introduces a result/purpose clause: بود ... تا بود هم indicates 'it was (so) that it would also be' — هم 'also' adds emphasis — and بدین یک نهاد is 'in this one disposition/form' (بدین = 'in this manner/this', here formed from ب + این). In context this clause states that the disposition was fixed to remain the same.

sentences.s006-s1: A lamp it is for me, greatly for the dark night; as far as you can, never turn toward evil.
sentences.s006-s2: When it has traversed a circuit of thirty days, the dark world becomes bright to it.
sentences.s006-s3: Then a thin and pale (appearance) appears, like the back of someone to whom the sorrow of love has befallen.
sentences.s006-s4: When the beholder saw his meeting from afar, at that same moment he too became invisible.
sentences.s006-s5: Another night will reveal more; it will give you more light.
sentences.s006-s6: In two weeks it will become wholly and properly complete, and it will return to the state it was in at first.
sentences.s006-s7: Each night it became thinner and drew nearer to the shining sun.
sentences.s006-s8: Thus the Lord gave it that disposition; so it was that it should remain in this one nature.
